name: TeamMoulton
tweet: So long as the filibuster remains in place, efforts to reform our gun laws, strengthen voting rights and address climate change will be held hostage by this undemocratic rule of Senate procedure. Give Americans the legislative voice they deserve. Abolish the filibuster.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: Throughout history, the filibuster was used to block landmark civil rights legislation, uphold segregation and deny protections for African Americans. It’s a relic of Jim Crow that has no place in our lawmaking process today.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: Republicans will say that the filibuster was created intentionally to make the Senate more bipartisan. But, that’s simply not true. Not only does the Constitution make no mention of this procedural wall, but it assumes that legislative decisions would be made by *majority* vote.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: The filibuster allows lawmakers elected by a minority of voters to exercise veto power over any and all laws-even on issues that the majority of the American people support. For instance, while nearly 70% of Americans support gun reform, we still may never see reform of any kind.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: To protect Americans from gun violence, we need to fundamentally change how the government works: We need to abolish the filibuster. Let me tell you why.  🧵

tweet: There's a lot we can do, but @senatemajldr and several Senators who think like him lack the moral courage to act and protect our communities. This is a dereliction of duty. Americans deserve a Congress that takes action on gun violence. 10/10   https://t.co/QfT7Cyxv76

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: The scale of gun violence in our country is staggering.  39,773 people are gunned down in our communities every year.  The CDC and public health researchers should get federal research funds & treat it like the public health emergency it is. The NRA has opposed that. 9/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: We should ban bump stocks, and other modifiers that allow someone to convert a legal weapon into an illegal one. Even President Trump is on board with this one—we should make his executive order permanent law. There are a few bills out there, I introduced a bipartisan one. 8/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: Congress must also pass an assault weapons ban.  I carried an assault rifle in Iraq. It literally saved my life. But there's no place in America where a weapon of war should be on our streets. 7/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: We should ban high-capacity magazines. Because if you can't hit what you're aiming for with 10 rounds, you certainly aren't trained well enough to use a gun. 6/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: We should create federal extreme risk protection orders—also known as a red flag law.  That's pretty straight forward—it says if a family member has concerns about the mental health of a loved one who owns a gun, they can report it and have the guns secured by the police. 5/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: There's also H.R. 1112—which gives law enforcement more time to do background checks. It's been sitting on the Senate calendar since March 5. Currently, if someone buys a gun and a background check isn't processed in 3 days, the buyer can pickup the gun 4/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: There's a pile of House-passed bills the Senate should take up. Like H.R. 8—legislation that we passed in February. This bill would expand background checks to close loopholes that let people buy guns without a background check at gun shows or through unlicensed dealers. 3/10

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "For all these reasons, I voted against this inadequate deal...[&] I’m working hard to bring new leadership to Washington. We can do better, and I'm confident that had this deal failed, we would've moved closer to addressing the many challenges our nation faces. That is our job."

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "What Democrats are asking from @SpeakerRyan is so simple: Just do your job and allow the House of Representatives to vote on an immigration plan. Vote how you want, but give us that chance. Give these innocent kids that chance. It’s the very least we should do.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "Yesterday, even Secretary Mattis, President Trump’s own Secretary of Defense, came out in support of DREAMers in our military, promising they won’t get deported. If someone inside the Trump Administration can stand strong, so should we.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "These innocent kids grew up in America, only know this country, and deserve some certainty for their futures. They serve in our military and are our kids’ friends in school. They, too, deserve a chance at the American Dream.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "And don’t sell America short – don’t think for a second that we can’t do these things and also take care of DREAMers, whom overwhelming majorities of Americans want to support.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "This is why the Republican tax cut for the rich is so dangerous for us all: it triples the deficit. And we can’t afford that. This deal makes it even worse.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "A good deal invests in our future. At times like this, most talk focuses on government spending. But equally important is the other half of the equation because we ultimately can’t pay our troops, pay our Medicare bills, and invest in our future without the funds to do so.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "A good deal supports our troops. As general after general has told us, short-term deals hobble our Pentagon planners & endanger our military. Only with a predictable budget, that is adequately funded, can we make investments in our troops & technology to meet the latest threats.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "Of course most Americans don’t have stocks, but rely on all the other investments we make: in education, health care, and in our local communities, especially after natural disasters. These things must be adequately funded.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "A good deal supports our economy. Just look at the reaction of the stock market over the past week as Republicans careened towards another shutdown.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "Rather than play politics with funding our government, pitting one group against another, we should make strong investments in our future.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: "I voted against the latest Continuing Resolution to fund the government because it shortchanged all the things we should be coming together, as both Democrats and Republicans, to accomplish for the people we represent.

name: TeamMoulton
tweet: @sethmoulton #BudgetDeal statement: Budgeting month to month and governing crisis to crisis is no way to run anything, let alone the United States of America. Republican leadership, if you can even call it that right now, is an embarrassment. America is better than this.

name: TeamMoulton
